generated_cells.notes: Title block with course context, section roadmap table, prerequisite and runtime notes.
pip install anthropic; imports numpy, torch, nn, F, matplotlib, ipywidgets, pandas, hashlib, os, time; version printout; CUDA availability notice; assert numpy >= 1.x.
Section 1 header with attention formula, LaTeX rendering, naive vs cached comparison table.
naive_attention_decode(): recomputes K and V for all past tokens each step; op_count grows as 2*(t+1) cumulative → O(T²). Full sanity check with seed 42 and identity weights.
cached_attention_decode(): projects only new token per step; op_count grows as +2 → O(T). Asserts np.allclose match with C04 output; prints both op count sequences.
build_naive_grid / build_cached_grid; side-by-side imshow with ListedColormap; cell labels R/N/C; legend patches; all assertions pass.
plot_flop_comparison(T): static run at T=16 then ipywidgets IntSlider (4–256, step 4, continuous_update=False); fill_between shaded wasted-work region; speedup ratio printed.
Section 2 header with LaTeX formula, Gemma 2 27B worked example, note on slide discrepancy (query heads vs KV heads), HBM/SRAM table.
DTYPE_BYTES dict; kv_cache_bytes_per_token(); max_tokens() with ValueError guard; Gemma 2 27B demo printing both slide value (num_heads=32) and GQA correct value (num_kv_heads=16); sanity asserts.
show_memory_interactive(): horizontal bar chart (3 context lengths, OOM coloring) + pie chart (weights/KV/free); ipywidgets with IntSliders and Dropdowns; continuous_update=False.
MODELS dict for 4 real models; barh chart with KB/token on x-axis; max-token annotations; OOM in red for GPT-3-175B on 40 GB; Llama-3-8B assertion verified.
Section 3 header; MHA/MQA/GQA comparison table; ASCII diagram of head-sharing patterns.

MultiHeadAttention, MultiQueryAttention, GroupedQueryAttention classes; MQA uses W_k/W_v of shape (d_model, head_dim) with broadcast; GQA uses repeat_interleave; kv_cache stored post-forward; all 9 shape assertions pass.
build_head_sharing_grid(); three-panel pcolormesh with tab10 colormap; KB/token subtitle using kv_cache_bytes_per_token; grid assertions pass.
plot_kv_reduction(num_kv_groups): guards non-divisor inputs with warning message; bar chart comparing GQA vs MHA bytes; regime label; IntSlider 1–8 continuous_update=False.
3-row DataFrame (MHA/GQA/MQA) for Llama-3-8B-like config; applymap/map compatibility shim for pandas < 2.1 vs >= 2.1; color_quality and color_retrain functions; row assertion.
Section 4 header; pricing table; stable-prefix-first design rule with ASCII layout diagram.

PrefixCacheSimulator class; MD5-hashed prefix lookup; cost at $2.50/$0.25 per 1M tokens; toy PrefixCacheSimulator assertions (hit_tokens==0 on first call, hit_tokens==5 on second); travel-agent demo with SYSTEM_PROMPT/USER1/USER2.
Textarea widgets for system+user prompts; Button with on_click; tokens_to_html() generates green/white span HTML; fresh PrefixCacheSimulator per click; error handling wraps entire on_click body.

LONG_SYSTEM_PROMPT (~500 words); measure_ttft() via Anthropic streaming API (text_stream, break after first chunk); guarded by try/except (ImportError, ValueError, Exception); skip message references C18–C19.
Summary markdown table with 8 methods × 4 columns; key insight callout.
summary_data list (8 rows); applymap/map compatibility shim; green/red/yellow coloring; dark header style; 3 assertions on data integrity.
5 key takeaways; further reading table with 5 arXiv links.

quality_checks.executability: Verified — no runtime dependencies beyond pip-installable packages; no GPU required; C20 gracefully skips without API key.
quality_checks.interactivity: 4 interactive cells: C07 (IntSlider, FLOPs), C10 (IntSliders+Dropdowns, memory), C15 (IntSlider, KV reduction), C19 (Textarea+Button, prefix cache visualizer).
quality_checks.visualizations: 6 matplotlib figures: C06 (imshow grids), C07 (line+fill), C10 (barh+pie), C11 (barh), C14 (pcolormesh × 3), C15 (bar).
quality_checks.assertions: 27 assert statements across code cells; all sanity checks execute before any interactive element.
quality_checks.pandas_compat: applymap/map compatibility shim applied in C16 and C22 to handle pandas < 2.1 and >= 2.1.

assumptions: Notebook targets Google Colab with Python 3.10+; all packages except 'anthropic' are pre-installed in standard Colab.
C20 (live API demo) requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY; the cell skips gracefully if absent and references simulation cells C18–C19 as fallback.
Word-split tokenization (str.split) in C18/C19 is intentionally naive — sufficient for demonstrating prefix matching without requiring tiktoken.
PyTorch attention in C13 does not apply a causal mask; full attention is used for shape/cache correctness demonstration.
Gemma 2 27B slide discrepancy (num_heads=32 vs num_kv_heads=16) is explicitly documented in C09 output with both values printed.
TTFT measurements in C20 include network latency; they demonstrate relative (warm vs cold) speedup rather than absolute values.
num_kv_groups slider in C15 allows non-divisors of 8 (e.g. 3, 5, 6, 7); the function prints a warning and returns early instead of crashing.
The summary table in C22 uses '?' for Sliding Window and Streaming LLM retraining requirement, matching the source lecture's slide 27.
